Package org.eventb.core.seqprover

DefaultCombinatorTests.TracingSuccess, DefaultCombinatorTests.TracingSuccess3, FiniteInclusionTac, FunAppInDomGoalTac, FunImageGoalAttempt, IdentityTactic, IdentityTactic.FailTactic, MapOvrGoalTac, MembershipGoalTac, NNFRewritesOnceTac, ParameterizedTactics.FakeTactic, ParameterizedTactics.SimpleTacWithParam, Tactics.FailureTactic, TestingAutoTactics.CombinedTestAutoTac, TestingAutoTactics.ParamTestAutoTac, TestingAutoTactics.SimpleTestAutoTac, TestingAutoTactics.TestProfile1, TotalDomToCProdTac, TracingReasoner.TracingTactic
public interface ITactic
Interface for tactics on proof trees.

Tactics are wrappers for proof tree modifications. Their purpose is to make operations on proof trees convenient to implement and combine.

This interface is intended to be implemented by clients.

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • BasicTactics

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Object
    apply(IProofTreeNode ptNode, IProofMonitor pm)
    Applies this tactic to a proof tree at a specific node.

  • Method Details

    • apply

      Object apply(IProofTreeNode ptNode, IProofMonitor pm)
      Applies this tactic to a proof tree at a specific node.

      The convention is that a tactic applied at a particular node is allowed to modify only the subtree rooted at that node, but this is not checked.

      Parameters:
      ptNode - The proof tree node at which this tactic should be applied
      pm - The proof monitor to monitor the progress of the tactic
      Returns:
      null iff the application was successfull.

      Note : The current convention for the return type is that in case the tactic was not successful, the proof tree is not modified, and the reason for tactic failure can be read from the toString() method from the object returned. In the near future the return type for the tactic will be refined to return more information useful for composing tactics. Implementors should therefore not rely on the return type at this time.

      See Also:
      • IProofMonitor
